academic_story.baseline_evidence: Synthetic Paper A: Reproducible Behavior Cloning for Multi-Intent NPC Control was reproduced as adjustment_minutes_per_scenario=45, intent_violation_rate=0.18, p95_decision_latency_ms=6.2, task_success_rate=0.71 within two percentage points or five percent relative
academic_story.expected_observation: intent_violation_rate should decrease from 0.18 toward 0.1; task_success_rate should increase from 0.71 toward 0.77; adjustment_minutes_per_scenario should decrease from 45 toward 30; p95_decision_latency_ms should hold from 6.2 toward 7.13
academic_story.gap: the baseline does not explicitly address shift raises policy uncertainty while the unconstrained action head has no explicit representation of scenario-valid actions under single-agent game environments with partially observed state and changing scenario rules
academic_story.implication: an uncertainty-triggered, action-constrained residual policy improves robustness and reduces adjustment work without sacrificing task success
academic_story.intervention: compose Semantic Action Mask, Uncertainty-Gated Residual Policy at explicit semantic and training boundaries
academic_story.mechanism: shift raises policy uncertainty while the unconstrained action head has no explicit representation of scenario-valid actions
academic_story.problem: reduce unintended NPC actions and manual rule adjustment under scenario shift: a reproduced behavior-cloning policy works on familiar scenes but emits invalid or intent-inconsistent actions after distribution shift

baseline.acceptance_tolerance: two percentage points or five percent relative
baseline.split: familiar training and validation, familiar plus shifted testing

expected_results.guardrail: task success must not decrease by more than 0.02
the gain must remain positive on shifted scenarios
latency remains within 15 percent of baseline

experiment_matrix.name: frozen-baseline
experiment_matrix.preprocessing: fixed observation normalization and action ontology version 1
experiment_matrix.purpose: reconfirm the reproduced baseline and modules-disabled parity under fixed evaluation code
experiment_matrix.split: familiar training and validation, familiar plus shifted testing
experiment_matrix.stopping_criteria: stop if the frozen baseline cannot be reproduced within tolerance; stop if a selected source is unverified or cannot be reused; stop if action identifiers or ordering cannot be aligned; stop if the full method does not reduce violations across four of five seeds; stop if p95 latency exceeds the 15 percent guardrail
experiment_matrix.tuning_budget: 20 trials per arm with the same early-stop budget
experiment_matrix.uncertainty_reporting: mean, standard deviation, and 95 percent bootstrap interval

innovation_points.contribution: couple semantic action masking with uncertainty-triggered residual correction so the baseline is preserved in familiar states and changed only for high-risk decisions
innovation_points.falsifiable_test: the contribution is unsupported if the full arm does not outperform the frozen baseline and strong comparison, or if single-module, leave-one-out, and interaction contrasts do not support the proposed mechanism
innovation_points.why_not_simple_splice: the mask and residual policy address different mechanisms and are connected by an explicit uncertainty trigger rather than unconditional stacking

limitations: expert data covers only familiar scenario combinations
the action head does not encode scenario-invalid actions
cannot identify semantically poor actions that remain technically valid
poor calibration may activate corrections too often
residual learning can overfit rare failure states
does not explicitly remove scenario-invalid actions

modules.adapter: a named action identifier join checks ontology coverage before filtering
assumptions: every baseline action identifier resolves before masking
modules.baseline_parity_behavior: disabling enable_semantic_action_mask preserves frozen baseline logits and action selection
modules.borrowed_component: pre-normalization action-validity mask with explicit state-to-action semantics
modules.compatibility_reason: semantic, shape, ordering, switch, gradient, update, loss-scale, and failure contracts are explicit
modules.failure_mode: an incomplete ontology may suppress a necessary action
modules.gradient_expectation: no gradient is introduced by the deterministic mask
modules.host_input_semantics: ordered action scores paired with a shared action ontology
modules.host_output_semantics: filtered scores over the unchanged action vocabulary
modules.insertion_point: the boundary after frozen action scores and before action selection
modules.loss_scale: not applicable because the module is non-trainable
modules.masks: binary scenario-valid action mask with explicit unknown-action rejection
modules.normalization: reuse frozen baseline logits before categorical normalization
modules.ordering: preserve the baseline action ontology order
modules.parameter_update_scope: no trainable parameters; baseline remains frozen
modules.predicted_effect: reduce intent violations caused by structurally invalid choices
modules.proposed_role: remove actions that are invalid in the current scenario
modules.semantic_mapping: the module uses the same action identifiers and ordering as the baseline
modules.source_input_semantics: current scenario state and discrete action ontology
modules.source_output_semantics: binary validity indicator for every action in the shared action vocabulary
modules.source_title: Synthetic Paper B: Semantic Invalid-Action Masking for Safe Policy Optimization

assumptions: uncertainty calibration uses only the training split
modules.baseline_parity_behavior: disabling enable_uncertainty_residual returns the unchanged frozen baseline action values
modules.borrowed_component: calibrated uncertainty gate and bounded residual-logit correction
modules.compatibility_reason: semantic, shape, ordering, switch, gradient, update, loss-scale, and failure contracts are explicit
modules.gradient_expectation: gradients update the residual and calibration heads but do not enter the frozen baseline encoder
modules.host_input_semantics: baseline features and ordered action values
modules.host_output_semantics: refined values with the same action order
modules.input_shape: batch x feature_dim plus batch x action_vocabulary
modules.insertion_point: the interface following baseline feature encoding
modules.loss_scale: residual loss weight 1.0 and calibration loss weight 0.1
modules.masks: respect the semantic action mask before residual action selection
modules.normalization: use frozen encoder statistics and temperature-calibrated uncertainty
modules.ordering: preserve the baseline action ontology order
modules.parameter_update_scope: residual policy and uncertainty calibration parameters only
modules.predicted_effect: improve task success in shifted scenarios
modules.semantic_mapping: source and host share action identifiers and value order
modules.source_input_semantics: frozen policy features, logits, and calibrated uncertainty
modules.source_output_semantics: bounded correction over the same ordered action vocabulary
modules.source_title: Synthetic Paper C: Uncertainty-Gated Residual Policies under Distribution Shift

references.title: Synthetic Paper A: Reproducible Behavior Cloning for Multi-Intent NPC Control
references.use_in_proposal: frozen baseline to reproduce before any modification

references.borrowed_component: pre-normalization action-validity mask with explicit state-to-action semantics
references.title: Synthetic Paper B: Semantic Invalid-Action Masking for Safe Policy Optimization
references.use_in_proposal: inserted at the boundary after frozen action scores and before action selection as remove actions that are invalid in the current scenario

references.borrowed_component: calibrated uncertainty gate and bounded residual-logit correction
references.title: Synthetic Paper C: Uncertainty-Gated Residual Policies under Distribution Shift
references.use_in_proposal: inserted at the interface following baseline feature encoding as refine decisions with high uncertainty

references.borrowed_component: full comparator implementation and fixed evaluation recipe
references.title: Synthetic Paper D: Shift-Robust Ensemble Policy for NPC Control

release_conditions: replace synthetic or unverified source cards with verified real-world evidence
run every frozen experiment arm from the fixed baseline and collect the declared resource measures
attach immutable evidence to every result represented as observed
complete domain-expert review before making scientific or novelty claims
stop_conditions: stop if the frozen baseline cannot be reproduced within tolerance
stop if a selected source is unverified or cannot be reused
stop if action identifiers or ordering cannot be aligned
stop if the full method does not reduce violations across four of five seeds
stop if p95 latency exceeds the 15 percent guardrail
strongest_reason: baseline, provenance, compatibility, hypothesis, comparisons, interaction analysis, and fair experiments passed the canonical audit
